architecture.summary: WebQA is a neurosymbolic program synthesis system that extracts information from structurally heterogeneous webpages. It combines pre-trained neural NLP models (keyword matching, question answering, entity extraction) with symbolic constructs for tree navigation and string manipulation in a domain-specific language (DSL). Given a natural language query, keywords, and a few labeled webpages, the system synthesizes DSL programs via optimal enumerative search that maximizes F1 score, then uses transductive learning over unlabeled webpages to select a single program with good generalization.

biblio.title: Web Question Answering with Neurosymbolic Program Synthesis

baseline.name: BERT-based QA system and wrapper induction techniques

claims.dataset: 25 different tasks across multiple domains on 160 webpages
claims.statement: WebQA significantly outperforms existing tools such as state-of-the-art question answering models and wrapper induction systems
The synthesis algorithm generates all DSL programs that achieve optimal F1 score on the training examples

The synthesis technique is compositional, prunes the search space by exploiting a monotonicity property of the DSL with respect to recall, and uses transductive learning to select programs with good generalization power

The approach combines pre-trained neural NLP models for keyword matching, question answering, and entity extraction with standard programming language constructs for string processing and tree traversal
Transductive learning generates soft labels for unlabeled webpages based on an ensemble of optimal candidate programs and selects the program whose output most closely matches the soft labels
Standard heuristics (e.g., based on program size) are not effective at distinguishing between the many (hundreds of) optimal programs on labeled data
